reviews.review_text: Janky as hell, but its honestly the best underwater/submarine survival game out there right now. and holy cow are there a ton of mods, and not just posters you can stick on submarine walls but full overhauls from warhammer to deadspace to fallout.

Probably the only game that can cause a team of 10 people to scream incoherently in panic at each other as teamwork disintegrates.

10/10 would be eaten by a Hammerhead 4000m deep in a Europan ocean again.

Where's the captain?

I was quite skeptical at first, either because of the graphics or mechanics (can't quite remember), but turns out Barotrauma is among the best games to play with friends I have seen so far (if you turn off friendly fire and traitors) otherwise it is the best way to hate them.
10/10, can't wait to play the new update

Terror. Fear. Paranoia. Camaraderie.

Playing with a crew of good friends was a genuine delight in teamwork and commiseration - after learning once more what the "End" part in "Endeorm" stands for.

Also, exploring the alien ruins gave me a palatable bout of claustrophobia and dread - which was an interesting thing to experience playing a 3rd-person, 2D action game with friends.

Fantastic game.
I instantly got hooked to the game, good progression and gameplay.

I love this game. 

Look up the song "Embrace the Abyss" from the Barotrauma OST and you'll hear the main page's theme. Unsettling is a great way to describe this game in a nutshell.

It's dark, creepy, makes my face wince constantly, and causes me to question why the fudge I'm even playing it.

But then I remember, this game is a lot like my own life experience.

Embrace the abyss and enjoy figuring out how to play this.

It's the first game I've ever played that seriously makes me want to play with other people. Sometimes the bots are so dumb they'll even remove their diving gear in 2000 + meters of Europan Ocean.

But then again, they may have simply wished to release themselves from the Hell I was putting them through.

10/10 would HIGHLY recommend.
This game like a long story from crew which can`t handle with crawler swarm attack to abyss monster hunters with only one goal: investigate the deep secrets of Europa.
This game is a hidden gem i played this on my laptop with no lag. Lore is really interesting and submarines look cool. This game is worth the money
This game is excellent. One of my favorite indie games ever. It's best, in my opinion, when things go hilariously wrong. 10/10

Barotrauma is an absolute masterpiece of a game. There is so much to do and explore while commanding your crew, playing with friends, or even building your own submarine from scratch. There are zero DLCs, zero microtransactions, and absolutely nothing stopping you from having a great time. This is one of those games where something you think is fine can go horribly wrong but still be funny and not upsetting. You don't "lose progress" meaninglessly and in my opinion there isn't any grinding. You're always doing something meaningful and interacting with the mechanics in a way that makes sense and gives you a challenge or something fun.

Note that this review is for the single-player experience.

I could see Barotrauma being engaging and fun in multiplayer. But I cannot recommend it to others who, like me, prefer single-player games. Playing as all of captain, medic, engineer, mechanic, and gunner, there are too many things to keep track of. The AI does an OK job at most of these, but you still need to manage all the various resources for each job. When docking at stations, running around to all the different merchants to stock up just feels like a chore.

In the submarine, I spent 95% of my time staring at the sonar. This feels like a waste, as I never get to see the monsters I encountered as anything other than blips on the sonar. Putting on the diving suit and going on extra-vehicular missions are the best part of the game, but doesn't make up for the rest for me.

Have only played this game single player and it's been a blast.

If you're looking for the type of game where your vessel/vehicle/etc is your lifeline and something you have to take care of and pilot, this scratches that itch.

 The advancement feels good and exploration feels as rewarding as it is unnerving

Gameplay as Solo, you can do 1-3 mission within ease, but after that everything just goes horipply wrong all the time and you are not sure what you need to do differently and you end up playing same mission on repeat without actually progressing.

Gameplay as Group you have better change to progress in game, but if you have enough players for group it start to become nightmare to get all those player play at the same time, and if even 1 player is missing their skills, recipes, or occupation is creating plot for a new disaster or just limiting your progress.

Submarine Editor is another disaster, UI isn't consumer friendly at all, you can't find anything, the "align on grid basically does nothing as every other object is miss aligned even with that buttons so you have to be tweaking constantly their orientation and placement. Game have know cost balance or any other calculation for statistics so you can creata/download most OP ship and put its prize on minimum destroying the games progression at same time. If you want to add oxygen, working ballast and electricity you need to watch entire youtube series to get grasp of it.

Why submarine editor isn't integrated in base game play loop? with modular submarines that you can tweak and upgrade?
It's not jank, the game itself is a cosmic entity with intentions beyond your understanding.
Great Game but not very beginner friendly causes most of my friends not to want to play

This is a positive review but i also have experienced VERY little of the game so this review is giving benefit of doubt for what i haven't seen, so as a game i like it, but i don't like a lot of the people this type of game attracts.
As someone who likes to buy games and never play them again, Barotrauma is a must have.
overdose on meth to have fun (meth gives a buff but theres smt called chem addiction) this game is awsome dude
this game still runs amazing even on a old pc and i have had alot of fun playing with my freinds i whold recmend this game if you like games like Subnutica

The game is very fun (with friends). 

There would be some difficulty in interacting with items and whatnot, but wouldn't affect too much cuz you'll get there eventually (like trying to interact with an item that's stacked because someone dumped everything on the floor at once)

Monsters are absolutely challenging which makes the game interesting. PvE submarine battle, outpost assault, among the interesting part of the game (again best with friends).

Never forget the Husk. The guy that talked went quiet only to find out he's gasping for air on the ground and after a quick check, he's infected and turned not long after. Fun times.
Best game like subnautica and terraria but has something unique about it, really good

the game is really fun and its something you can come back too whenever and its genuinely a lot more fun with other people and friends but even in single player its still immensely fun, definitely worth the price point and a game you can consistently come back too
Incredible game! Somehow this ended up as my third most played game in my library. Completely blew my expectations out of the water (haha)
its a bit confusing at times, the quest system is a bit goofy and sometimes theres not enough of a material in the "go mine this" quests, but over all its extremely fun!

this game is AMAZING and distracts me from streaming to my 0 daily viewers, but i do feel a couple items could be buffed
safety harness- its description is it resists a hull breach, but doesnt really, only against intruders, i think it should resist some pressure and organ damage 
flashlight - should make more need for light outside of a diving suits natural headlight, maybe buff it?
imagine a game where you get to live the experience that simon went through in the iron lung. pretend iron lung the game doesn't exist. this is the next best thing.
very cool mix of a submarine simulation game and horror. love the art style. more fun with friends than single player though but still pretty fun alone with ai.
Great game to play with a crew. It's a mix between engineering and survival horror that is actually fun. Can't really recommended it enough, it was an experience.
I played barotrauma in my first 50 hours and i was like "what the hell is going on" Then it was the most fun game i ever clicked on.

This is an extremely unique game that, honestly, completely blindsided me for how much i enjoyed it. In fact, "enjoyed" really isn't the word for it. This game is genuinely the first time I've felt straight up addicted to a game; and i don't mean alcahol addiction either, this game is (fittingly) straight fent for your brain and soul. I haven't put this kind of time into a game over such a short period since elden ring, and i used up PTO for that.

It's a game with (once again, fittingly) a fantastic mix of great depth and complexity, as well as a loving serving of jank to keep it light. The world is very fun, and honestly i may be extremely biased toward the setting, as it's one of my all time favorites. I absolutely adore the undersea/space hybrid vibe the game has, and the story elements are light but very fun and interesting, at least to me.

but, the reason you're here is for the moment to moment gameplay, which has this great quality of being consistently tense while very rarely feeling unsalvageable. there just aren't a lot of encounters you couldn't technically get yourself out of if you know how... But that experience of knowing how to un-♥♥♥♥ yourself only comes with playing the game enough to learn it inside and out, which is a very fun process in and of itself.

as a last note, and maybe the most important one, the mod support is fantastic. This is definitely one of those games that can definitely be played without mods... but really shouldn't be. there's just so much variety and so much high quality goodness out there, that you really should just get out there and get a few. I highly recommend checking the workshop page out before you buy if you're on the fence; look at the top rated of all time, and you'll see just how strong the mod variety is. (I personally love the "Atlas' shipyards" collection, and the "anime waifu" mod... naturally ;P)

All in all, this truly is a 10/10 game for me, which is insane for me to say. I genuinely told myself i might refund it if i didn't like it, and got it on sale for half off. Little did i know i was getting myself into a genuinely one of a kind gaming experience that isn't quite touched by anything else. I enjoy every aspect so much, i really can't think of a single real complaint. The only sadness this game leaves me with is wishing there were more...

Very fun/tense campaign which is great in singleplayer and multiplayer, especially when chaos breaks loose. Has a good level of technical depth but is also not too horrible to get into as a newcomer, just don't be surprised if you get mauled in your first encounter by an unexpected swarm in a cave, or possibly something bigger.

Zone out for 5 seconds and flora has taken over my ship, eight mudraptors have breached the hull, the reactor power is out and is on fire, my Assistant just put fourteen timed explosives and shot multiple mini-nukes into the medbay, and half my friends are dead. W game fr

Barotrauma is a wonderful experience. Hours spent with friends panicking at the bottom of the ocean. Do pick this up if you enjoy brutal tension spikes though. The game has a steep learning curve, the first 20-odd hours you'll just be confused, then you learn the secrets. This game is amazing, janky and a mess. 10/10
Probably the best concept for a game ever, and it more than delivers. This game hooked me faster than an Endworm will hook your poor Dugong. 10/10

Though the game looks very fun and I was looking forward to play with my friends. It does not run well on Linux play via Steam. The menus constantly crash and the game stutters. It is effectively unplayable despite me trying to get it to work more than any other game I have running. I can't recommend it if you have Linux and hope better support in the future.
genuinely one of the funniest games I've ever played. Only criticism is that lategame multiplayer was straight unplayable on my end, far too laggy. But still an absolute blast.

game is extremely fun and chaotic with friends, the sea creatures freak me out cuz i dont like the deep ocean and im scared, which is a good thing. the game got a bunch of mechanics and the mods add even more to the game
Very fun and engaging game with so much to do in such a small game.
This game made me soil my pants atleast 10 times now, would reccomend, though keep new pants close.
Amazing game I love the atmosphere, the ragdoll physics, the array of creatures. Insanely W game and W devs.
I love barotrauma. Peak gameplay if you wanna play solo or multiplayer. I haven't even tried modded yet but vanilla is enough to satisfy me.
Very decent game with its own quirks is very unforgiving of mistakes and makes resource management a must.

This is an absolutely solid game, and very much fulfills the potential of the design and mechanics. I highly recommend this game, but for very specific people. It relatively is niche in style and play, but executes it very well.

The devs took the premise of this game: a dark, sidescroller, submarine, co-op horror and adventure game and took it to nearly the peak of what it could be.

This is a rare Early Access success. Delivered complete with active bug fixes, support and community.

The screen shots and videos of gameplay are very accurate and representative, so if you think you might like it, you probably will. It is also very customizable, with built-in sub editor to completely customize your experience, and has good mod support (I observed this, but I have never played with mods).

The bugs which I reported were addressed relatively quickly, with both dev-assisted work arounds and then fixes in the following update (this was years ago). They still actively support the game, and you can see Regalis11 opening and actively working on bugs on github:
https://github.com/FakeFishGames/Barotrauma/issues/

Regalis11 fixed my personal bug complaints back in 2022. 4 years of transparent bug reports and fixes. Buy this dev a coffee.

For this transparency and directness, I'm tempted to buy the DLC which I don't actually need. I don't need "Home & Harbor" DLC, because the Submarine editor they built into the game is enough for me to make the submarine of my dreams that feels like home. I might still buy it.

My initial biggest issue with the game was the dark, grungy, gritty aesthetic. and rough sidescroller controls. For me, it's rough to look at for long periods, but with the Submarine Editor, I can just make my own sub completely to my own aesthetic tastes. I also love to build and design, so on my most recent pick-up of the game, I have spent about 4 times as much time editing as actually playing. Maybe if my sub is ever good enough, I'll upload it to the workshop.

I hope this review is exactingly accurate. So the bad, then the good:

So, my (minor) problems with the game:
1. Long, tedious missions sometimes (you can increase the difficulty and play with friends to mitigate this). My poor gunners get bored, even as I drive as fast as I can.
2. RNG missions are a bit disappointing sometimes in the campaign. You won't always get 3 good missions in the direction you want to travel. It's fine for an hour or two of play, but feels lackluster for binge-playing.
3. The event system uses hidden skill checks for success, and the skill used is not always obvious. Really annoying to just hit the unceremonious end/failure because you didn't have electrical skill and talked to the quest NPC with your captain. You can look up these events if it bothers you, minimal rewards. I'd like to see this overhauled and dramatically expanded.
4. The AI is complex, but can be absolutely useless in particular contexts, and is reliant upon all other systems (submarine waypoints) being bug-free. They cannot easily unjam themselves or get out of behaviour loops.
5. Minerals and similar can (rarely) spawn trapped in the map walls because of procedural generation (map walls can pinch around the minerals).
6. Dark, rough, grungy, gritty aesthetic is a bit much some days for me (personal, not everyone).
7. Sidescroller mouse controls can feel a bit janky if I haven't played for a few months
8. Outposts/colonies are sometimes tough to navigate, especially when tired (procedural generation I think, also player skill issue)
9. Visual bugs because of everything being assembled from sprites; clipping, gaps, overlaps, tiny one or two pixel ick everywhere when you look. If you build your own sub, you can mitigate 80% of this.

That's it. Minimal complaints. Only a couple genuine frustrations. 

So the good parts:
1. Great co-op horror and mission-based game. It has all your standard RPG quest styles: hunt/kill monsters, clear monster nest, raid a NPC outpost, fight enemy sub, explore and loot wrecks/caves, mine minerals, transport cargo/escort NPC (only fun when augmenting other dangerous missions), recover relics, and a few more.
2. Combat feels very unique and particular to this game, but feels cohesive. As does the extensive medical system.
3. IT FEELS COMPLETE. This should not be an achievement these days, but you as a player know EXACTLY what I mean.
4. The wiring logic system is about the best I've every played (except for components like adders only having 2 inputs; you must cascade and stack them). Anything from reactors to periscopes to doors can be wired and controlled through logic. The circuit box addition was a blessing for submarine designers. The devs listen.
5. Everything that isn't nailed down (part of a sub/outpost wall, fixture or installation) is able to be both crafted and deconstructed.
6. You can fix (in-game vanilla) or mod most things that bother you.
7. In-depth build system and ability to save copies of Vanilla subs and edit them.
8. a) The devs listen. The devs fix. This was out of early access in 2023, with 3 years of near-seasonal updates. Who does this nowadays?
b) I can see the gosh darn source code? What the actual heck?

The economy pricing sometimes feels scuffed, sometimes to your benefit and sometimes not. Should a particular material cost 400mk? Probably not, but we can afford it because we transported some clown masks.

Again, the devs took the premise of this game: a dark, sidescroller, submarine, co-op horror and adventure game and took it to nearly the peak of what it could be. There is a huge variety of missions, environments, hazards, enemies, monsters, submarines, weapons, and no limit with custom subs and logic circuits. The components - memory, concatenation, RegEx (puke), and basic logic components make the system Turing-complete. If I choose, I can make my own autopilot system for a sub MID-GAME as a sub retrofit. I cannot explain how satisfying the building aspects are.

I don't remember what I paid for this, but it is a 40$ game, easily.

I look through my library of games that went through Steam's Early Access, and I weep for the game worlds that could have been if all Early Access titles had this level of dev support.

I'll recommend this game since I definitely got my money out of it, but it took a bunch of mods and dealing with a ridiculous amount of jank to be playable. This game needs a huge bug/qol patch but the dev is glacial in getting anything resembling a patch out, expecting the mod devs to fix everything. It really feels early access, but it's not.
Bit challenging at first but fun once you figure stuff out, runs well too.
great game.  8/10.  The video and subject don't give it justice to the magnificence of the chaos.  Can't wait to try game chat.

just clowning around before dying either from an eldrich monster or the captains terrible driving.

this game is really fun, would recommend, and it has a sizable mod workshop
fun to play, but mostly because of great friends.
Very detailed complex game with a steep learning curve. The game is at its best in multiplayer. Half of the fun comes from goofing off with your crewmates.

W game one of the best games i ever played.
there are some kinks here and there but i still love this game.

I LOVE IT. its great, its creepy, its unnerving and its freaky, this game is like if rimworld and kenshi had a baby that they then send to navy school to become a sub operator, only for that kid to bump his head and end up with some fkdup kind of psychosis and now you live in this childs version of underwater hell, diving to depts so dark that it makes you worry more about what is down there with you rather than the darkness itself, ALL OF IT, I love all ofit, im not going to elaborate onit, I genuinely highly recommend any one that is curios, to give this game a try, and you will know within 1-2 hours whether or not you like this game (and more :) ), and as a side note it has coop, online multiplayer servers and the modding scene is fantastic. (I highly recommend going in vanilla to get the grips and then later on if you want to mod, 100 percent check out baroverhaul mod *chefs kiss* very good mod )
Playing single-player. Every time I'm controlling someone else My captain books it to the medical cabinet and when I'm back there vomiting and suffering oppoite addition. 10/10
So fun. The gameplay is complicated and unintuitive at times, but the atmosphere and difficulty are amazing. Highly recommend.
After i got V1 i alway just use dual sword to kill everything from normal monster to deep monster and even last boss but i die to cyanide
Doable and fun solo but at least one other person makes all the difference. This game is quite evil fair warning.
Good game, don't use tutorials, learning the hard way is fun

Stress, scares, and subs.

A wonderfully fun experience that I thoroughly enjoyed. Barotrauma is a submarine game based on Europa where you and your crew have to survive all the horrors found in the depths of the Europan ocean. 

The experience is both fun and terrifying with both the environment and your "fellow" humans trying to kill you. Most of my hours are spent in multiplayer where we had a person for everything. Even though I mostly played with mods, I still had a blast with all those stressful experiences of abyssal creatures trying to sink your submarine and your crew trying to keep it afloat. 

Even though I logged most my hours in multiplayer, the single player experience was still just as enjoyable. I still highly recommend trying out multiplayer even if you don't have friends to play with. Most of the community is very helpful and welcoming to new players, though be prepared to download mods because most multiplayer sessions are modded. 

TL;DR: Fun scary submarine co-op game.

Fun, wanted to try this because I enjoyed playing Faster Than Light and wanted the same kind of experience but as a multiplayer. Not saying they're the same game- but the gameplay of "go here, don't die. lmao you did anyway" is there and is what I want.

Not disappointed at all, a little learning curve- but nothing a quick run of the campaign doesn't fix. (not even finished yet.)

♥♥♥♥ ballast flora. Wholeheartedly.

I love this game. It is addictive. Even for an "I hate multiplayer" guy like me.

Already have 500+ hours in it and I haven't even started fiddling with the submarine builder and the automation.
Initially I was quite affraid how can I possibly operate a submarine which requires at least 5 people crew. This even lead to this game being left unplayed at in my library for more than an year. When I finally gave it a try, to my surprise the bots were quite competent (in most cases)* and it is actually quite easy to operate huge crews - I have at least 15 bots in all my playthroughs.

* - ok, don't yell at me. I am working with microslop cret!ns and as a result my standards are really low.
I didnt think it was my kind of game but im sure heroin addicts said the same thing about heroin But now I find myself wrapping a belt around my arm before launching the game

I was eyeing this for some time... the 2D arrow drive was quite a turndown for me. I am  glad that I finally got the game despite,  and learned how cool it is. 

I like
- very deep mechanics. Do not let the graphics make you assume this is a shallow mobile-like game. You will be crafting, repairing, training, mining, salvaging, fighting, designing subs  (!!). If you into control technology, you will enjoy designing the control system for the sub.
- Dynamic mission system
- Lots of vanilla subs, more in the workshop
- Managenent aspec, you need to make sure ther is fuel for the reactor, for the welders,  there are medical supplies, and ammo for the main weapons.
- Very complex submarines with working dive supporting airlocks (you need to swim out from the sub)
- Side quests, like rundown signal beacon . This is my favorite, you need to fix  up flooded, malfunctiononing beacon stations. Fix the hull, power up the reactor, pump out the water and fix wiring, components.
- Crew management aspect, there besides the captain, there are many roles in the crew
- Fully  single player capable
- Extensive procedurally generated world
- First I hated the oversimplified 2D arrow drive.. but found out its fun. Pretty similar to Ostranauts, loos lame but actually quite fun to do the oversimplified get-around mechanism.
- Complex item logic, like electric equipment needs battery replacement, you can charge the depleted cells.

Dislike
- Lame economic system. Starter boat costs 5K with 2 turrets, fully functional submarine with reactor, ballast  tanks, main propulsion and control system. Then it is like 4K to add an extra turret.
- You spend on your starter  boat... get the hull upgraded, extra turret, upgrade many modules... then  you find out you just need a bigger boat.
- Huge miss of lacking hunger / bladder and sleep mechanics.
- Some rundown stations have top mount docking which implies sub having bottom docking, which is not the case. 
- Hull is too soft. The game is made around creatures actually  breaking into the sub... they should bonk into it, damage it, make the crew injured, make it leak but tearing through the hull.. nah
- too much of the "you are blind" feeling when helming the sub. Weapon officers look out through periscopes, but you got none. There should be a driving periscope. No info about the electrical system. I do not  see how much the reactor is making, how much the propulsion is taking up.
- the subs systems draw too much power in idle. Without propulsion it should not be more than 10%. It literally eats  through the fuel while I need the engineer on an expedition so they cannot replace fuel.
- I should be able to put more fuel in, those should be exhausted uniformly and it should be installed fuel rod times slower to deplete. What is the point of the reactor having multiple fuel slots if it just overheats?
- Fuel should be like 10 times more expensive, and should last 15 times longer.
- Subs are too cheap, should be min. 30K... if one turret costs at least 4K, the whole submarine should be way more. I should be able to sell a submarine, so it is not a waste to upgrade the starter sub.
- The  game overgrown the single sub-item logic... like the diving suit only has oxygen tank, yet it should have a battery as well to replace.
- It would make sooo much sense to load a vanilla sub, and SAVE AS .. it on a different name. I just need to tweak some stuff, I do not want to build one from scratch.
- Boat designer is detached from the game. It should be started by interacting with a shipyard NPC for a cost. There is so much potential in customziing a submaring, yet so inaccessible. I do not want to design a  new sub but upgrade and customize a vanilla sub. 
- Lame crew control... most similar games have a table where X axis contains all activities, eg. refueling reactor, controlling reactor, repair electric devices... etc. and Y axis contains crew members. Writing numbers into cells define priority to do that task. 

Bottom line, this is an awesome game, with doable learning curve. Looks shallow first look, but it is not! Careful with the difficulty though.

This game is a amazing. it is one of the best 2D horror games i have found on steam. you can get the feeling of terror wile also being able to become a tank. and with modding, you can bring the game from a 6 to a 10. so if you can get passed being a week mariner at the start enjoy the rest of the game. don't let the crawlers bite.

I love the game but can be janky at times. Even when I'm the host, in multiplayer dropping through floors will randomly cause me to fall repeatedly and I'll fall over and over until I take a little bit of blunt force. Doesn't ever happen to me if I'm using a ladder though. If you run off stairs a little too fast, you might end up propelling yourself so far you take fall damage.

That said it's still a very enjoyable game. Has a perk tree that allows you to specialize into the content you actually want to touch on and customize the role you want to play in your crew. Me personally I love playing medic and going into gene splicing to buff my crew, and going down husk symbiosis path. You never have to bother much actually being a medic if you just make them all regenerate enough they don't die, then just inject them with drugs before combat. 

Some say the later game can feel a bit of a slog, but I enjoy grinding out missions and figuring out how to get through them with less issues.

Bots aren't nearly as bad as they used to be, you can pretty consistently have a bot crew that can repair decently and security officer bots will land most shots. However, I'd definitely never take one outside the sub with me, definitely more fun with friends.

There is then the secondary game within a game, the sub editor. I love creating my own custom submarine's to then use, it's very enjoyable. Also extremely fun if you like wiring custom logic and so on, as you can include it in your submarine that you create.

Overall very fun and enjoyable for me, definitely can feel a bit jank sometimes. However it's never to a point it has made me feel it's a bad game, just maybe the physics are a bit funny. Would be awesome if after the new DLC settles in, they consider making a second game, taking the experience they have know and doing it again but much better. I'd be likely to buy it.
although there is a lot that I don't know about the game, the basics were easier to learn than I expected
This game is intense but fun once you learn all the different pos and the creatures are well made so yeah i rec this game for all horror fans! Enjoy.

The game has a suspenseful feel and gruesome monsters that just hit different

The procedural generation makes it endlessly replayable

And every decision can matter one wrong turn into a rock can spell doom for you and your crew

Never a dull moment. Always on edge for the next creature  to come along.

Keep it up barotrauma. 

Youve got my support

whether you wish to command the ship, repair its hull covered in grease, get zapped trying to screw somthing, combat enemies with blade or bullet, poison or even alter DNA, or be expendable. this game is perfect, you have your ship, and 4 (soon to be 5) factions to help

you have the europa coalition (government)
jovial sepratists (rebels)
church of the husk (cult)
and children of the honkmother (cult)

play the game serious, silly, pve or pvp or somtimes both at once if your allies are clown cultists. 

get this game, talk about it, it is amazing. the husk commands it.

I got Barotrauma a week ago only having heard about it vaguely before, thinking: "Hmm, why not give it a try?"
A week. I haven't shut down the PC, i have hardly even closed the game at all, i just pass out while playing roughly once daily. It's been years since i've been captured in this way by a game.

Really though: Imagine Happy wheels suddenly experienced traumatically life altering events beyond it's comprehension, and the only affordable therapist is H.P. Lovecrafts sleep paralysis demon who coincidentally hates it with a burning passion.

That is Barotrauma. 
It won't be kind, but it will be rewarding. It will be interesting. Its claustrophobic design in and outside of your submarine instill a sense of paranoia that is only fueled by the constant threat of... everything. Injury, Sea monsters, clowns, bad financial decisions... 
Especially bad financial decisions.

In all seriousness, the ragdolly 2D mechanics may seem Happy-wheel esque at first, but they somehow only add to the disorienting feel of constant doom. 

The lore to this game is equally fleshed out and the modding community is... bustling but maybe a bit unchecked, in a good way?

Gameplay ist mostly structured in customizable roles with assignable tasks, so i would absolutely recommend watching a tutorial video or doing the training missions, because this game will not take you by the hand. 
This is especially true in Multiplayer, but the community seems to be very welcoming toward beginners and always happy to explain.

All i wanted was to check out what i believed to be this cool little "pixel game" , and now my therapist and i are discussing the dangers of self-harmingly-difficult-games.
 
9/10 had to up my meds, especially since i unluckily got it 3 days before a 50% sale

Get it, or it will get you
Fighting deep sea monsters and your char opioid addiction is a struggle... but really, the real enemy is my own inability to quit playing this thing. 5/5

Its a super fun game but certainly has a bit of an intimidating learning curve. Some of the crews and jobs can be confusing and at the start there's a ton to take in, adding in the fact that the background itself is complicated things can get messy quick. I recommend you and some friends hop in a submarine and just try to get from one base to another. You'll learn as you go and honestly I found that to be the most fun way to play.

Whole crew of bots died, only npc left was my security officer. Ship was completely flooded and recked and as I was trying to repair, despite the very common electrical shocks and horrible condition of his health, he kept muttering to himself soft platitudes like "everything is going to be alright" or "we still got this." I actually did manage to repair enough to get to a dock, and from then on he was my main guy who I would sacrifice everyone else for. Incredible. But honestly, cool game, single player is okay but multiplayer is where it really takes off.

This game can be extremely fun with the right playstyle and knowledge, but it can also be extremely frustrating if you don't know what you are doing, and im not talking about just game mechanics, but moreso the way the game is designed.

Personally I think the campaign just isn't designed well. It's by no means unplayable but I have infinitely more fun in the game when I play the other games modes included (PvP, Sandbox and Mission). 

The game has like near infinite mod support, so it's probably worth the $35 price tag.

Solid buy, but campaign needs some work.

Feels like I was just dropped into the campaign without any real instruction besides "do missions". Crashes frequently on Mac, especially during multiplayer and NPC crewmates are hard to control. Great fun with friends though.

When I first got the game (a friend asked me to get it for us after seeing a youtube video) I took the tutorial and thought it was way too complicated. I spent several minutes trying to figure out where the oxygen tanks were on a diving suit locker. After this, I stopped playing for I think around a year.

Then one day I saw a youtube video, thought the game looked cool, and when I looked to see what the game is I realized I still had it in my library. Once I started to actually play the game some more, join some public lobbies, and mess around with it, I discovered that it was indeed way too complicated. But that's perfectly fine because it's a team game. You don't need to know everything about the game or try to play every class at once, because your teammates can usually get you through some missions, and along the way you'll slowly learn everything there is to know about the game.

700 hours later, I've fought endworms with swords and lived to tell the tale, I've ascended to true symbiosis with the church of the husk, I've blown up many nuclear reactors, and I have slain god. There is such a massive amount of things you can do and a nearly endless amount of replayability, and I'm certain I'll get at least another 700 hours out of the game.

Even if you do eventually get bored of what Vanilla has to offer (which I have not yet) there's a massive amount of mods and custom submarines that I've only just dipped my toes into. The game might be a little pricey for me, but that's really just me being stingy, I would say this is just as good as any number of $60 games. I've found myself playing Barotrauma 10x more than Hitman WOA. I really cannot recommend the game enough.
Good game for game who have fear of big daddy in water and the vast ocean itself.

"THE ONLY GOOD MARINE IS A SUB-MARINE!"
Barotrauma is a co-op survival drowning sim that will test both your ability to work with a team and your judgement in ways other games simply cannot. Fight together, or drown trying.

There's a surprising amount of *depth* to Barotrauma, to the tens of drugs you can administer or abuse, to the multitudes of ships you can pilot, to the wiring system allowing you to do basically anything while on the ship.
If you somehow grow tired, you can make your own outposts and subs in the same editor the real devs use in order to create the many ships in game. If even that's not enough, the game has Workshop support built in, too.

If you got a ton of friends or even if you're on your own, Baro is a really cool game with a lot to share.

I've played this game with multiple friend groups and none of us have ever figured out how to play this game but regardless, this game is an absolute blast. We still keep coming back to this game just because of the craziness.

Genuinly such a fun game, I havent been able to stop playing theres so much to learn and do while still being approachable. The ship editor is amazing, and the multiplayer is a blast... if you get good players, which is super rare.
